response:
To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & -12 & 13 \\ -6 & 13 & 11 \\ -7 & -6 & -8 \end{pmatrix} $$

The characteristic polynomial $p(\lambda)$ is defined as:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) = \det \begin{pmatrix} 3-\lambda & -12 & 13 \\ -6 & 13-\lambda & 11 \\ -7 & -6 & -8-\lambda \end{pmatrix} $$

We will expand this determinant along the first row:
$$ p(\lambda) = (3-\lambda) \begin{vmatrix} 13-\lambda & 11 \\ -6 & -8-\lambda \end{vmatrix} - (-12) \begin{vmatrix} -6 & 11 \\ -7 & -8-\lambda \end{vmatrix} + 13 \begin{vmatrix} -6 & 13-\lambda \\ -7 & -6 \end{vmatrix} $$

**Step 1: Calculate the $2 \times 2$ minors.**

1.  First minor:
    $$ \begin{vmatrix} 13-\lambda & 11 \\ -6 & -8-\lambda \end{vmatrix} = (13-\lambda)(-8-\lambda) - (11)(-6) $$
    $$ = -(13-\lambda)(8+\lambda) + 66 $$
    $$ = -(104 + 13\lambda - 8\lambda - \lambda^2) + 66 $$
    $$ = -104 - 5\lambda + \lambda^2 + 66 $$
    $$ = \lambda^2 - 5\lambda - 38 $$

2.  Second minor:
    $$ \begin{vmatrix} -6 & 11 \\ -7 & -8-\lambda \end{vmatrix} = (-6)(-8-\lambda) - (11)(-7) $$
